title: Understanding the AI economy
excerpt: We’re releasing the first Activity, Task, Landscape, and Adoption Study (ATLAS) report, showing how people use Google’s AI tools.
content_html:
Jul 23, 2026

|

Google’s ATLAS is an expansive look at how people are using AI at work and in day-to-day life.

Zanna Iscenko

AI & Economy Lead, Chief Economist's Office

Scott Strand

Head of StratOps and Special Projects, Technology & Society

Your browser does not support the audio element.

Listen to article

[[duration]] minutes

This content is generated by Google AI. Generative AI is experimental

There is broad agreement that AI’s potential to transform the global economy and the way we work is significant. However, the outcomes – what this means for work, for people’s lives, and the economy writ large – are not automatic nor guaranteed. A lot has to happen. To get there, we as a society must work together to positively shape how AI impacts our lives, jobs, and economy. In order for this shared work to be effective, it is critical to have a rich understanding of how AI is being adopted and used in the economy. Society needs empirical insights and evidence-based research to inform decisions, initiatives, and actions.

To help, Google is launching the first iteration of the AI & Economy ATLAS (Activity, Task, Landscape, and Adoption Study), an ongoing, large-scale, de-identified study of how people are using Google’s AI products and tools. ATLAS’s first dataset (v1.0) is built from 15 million aggregated and de-identified human-AI interactions across the Gemini App, AI Mode, and the Gemini API, which together are used by more than 1 billion people monthly. ATLAS v1.0 insights span more than 150 countries, 140 languages, 800 occupations, and 4,000 tasks; ATLAS is the most comprehensive look to date at how real people are using AI at scale.

ATLAS sheds light on how people are using Google’s AI tools for various tasks at work and in their day-to-day lives. The ATLAS v1.0 report provides an early view of a quickly moving landscape: AI’s capabilities are advancing, its use is evolving, and tools for observing its impact on the economy are still a work-in-progress.

What are we learning from ATLAS v1.0?

Here a few of the most interesting observations so far:

  • AI use at work is broad but shallow: Workplace adoption spans all industry sectors and also 68% of all occupations that collectively represent 90% of total U.S. employment. However within jobs, people are using AI selectively: in a typical job AI is used for only ~21% of tasks.
  • At work, most AI use is focused on collaboration and assistance with tasks, and so far task automation is uncommon: ATLAS data shows the vast majority of AI interactions at work focus on collaborative uses such as ideation, strategy, information retrieval, and learning. Tasks like creative design and hypothesis testing (categorized in ATLAS as “non-routine cognitive”) show up in AI work interactions at a much higher rate than in the economy as a whole (65% vs 35%). Less than 10% of those interactions fully automate tasks.
  • AI use is not limited to white collar workers, it’s also assisting workers in predominantly physical and manual occupations with adjacent tasks: AI use for work is not limited to jobs traditionally seen as knowledge work. While not as prevalent, workers in manual and technical trades (e.g., auto technicians, industrial mechanics) are using conversational AI as a live collaborator for real-time diagnostics, troubleshooting, and on-the-fly learning. When workers in these areas use our AI tools, they’re 2x more likely to use multimodal AI (i.e. using AI to create images or video). For example, automotive technicians and industrial mechanics use AI to interpret complex test results, debug electrical wiring, and inspect machinery for wear.
  • AI is delivering value at home that may be missed in standard economic metrics, particularly around high-friction administrative tasks: Over 86% of interactions with AI tools in ATLAS occur outside of work. People are using AI in new and interesting ways not captured in standard economic metrics including productive household activities (e.g. researching purchases, help with using appliances, and tools) and high-friction administrative tasks (e.g. navigating government services like taxes, licensing, and fines).
  • Global AI adoption is tracking GDP per capita, with notable exceptions: AI usage has diffused globally. ATLAS data shows AI usage in over 150 countries and territories that represent 99% of the world's population. We also see this in the diversity of languages used in ATLAS. English represents only about a third of global AI conversations, and users do not systematically abandon their native languages for complex tasks. Looking more deeply, on a per-capita basis AI usage closely mirrors a country’s relative level of wealth, raising concerns about a persisting digital divide. However this isn’t a universal rule: some middle-income countries in South America and the Middle East are adopting AI at rates comparable to higher-income countries.

Here are some additional findings:

How ATLAS has been developed

ATLAS insights are powered by Google DeepMind’s Observation Clustering and Taxonomy Organisation (OCTO), a tool for transforming massive unstructured text data, like LLM conversations, and distilling them into organized entities. ATLAS has been developed with the highest level of privacy protections. In addition to scrubbing personally identifiable information (PII), we added several additional layers of protection that automatically remove any possible references to sensitive information, remove all linkages between de-identified ATLAS data and underlying user logs, summarize the text data, and aggregate summaries into groups representing multiple users.

What’s next?

This is just the beginning — the ATLAS v1.0 represents the start of a long-term project. AI’s capabilities continue to expand, people are continuing to find new and interesting ways to use it, and research methodologies to understand AI continue to evolve. There are many more questions around AI and the economy where more work will be needed — work that Google’s AI & Economy Research Program is undertaking in collaboration with academic and other researchers.

And there is a much wider range of economically-relevant AI usage not reflected in ATLAS. These include AI-enabled products with billions of users and interactions like Google Workspace, Google Translate, and AI Overviews; enterprise platforms, such as Gemini for Google Cloud and Gemini Enterprise; and frontier capabilities in several key areas, such as agentic coding and world models.

As we continue to build upon ATLAS and expand its scope and capabilities to generate new insights, we aim to provide a sharper understanding of the AI-driven transformation of the economy. We hope it is helpful to researchers, policymakers, businesses, workers, and other participants in the economy as we work together to shape the ways AI can positively support people in their lives.

We’d like to acknowledge Dame Diane Coyle (Cambridge) and Dr. David Autor (MIT) for their contributions to the ATLAS v1.0 report, as well as the entire ATLAS team 1 .

sentences.en: Society needs empirical insights and evidence-based research to inform decisions, initiatives, and actions.
sentences.zh: 社会需要经验见解和循证研究来为决策、举措和行动提供信息。

sentences.en: ATLAS data shows AI usage in over 150 countries and territories that represent 99% of the world's population.

sentences.zh: 我们在 ATLAS 使用的语言多样性中也看到了这一点。

sentences.zh: 英语仅占全球人工智能对话的三分之一左右，用户不会系统性地放弃母语来完成复杂的任务。

sentences.en: Looking more deeply, on a per-capita basis AI usage closely mirrors a country’s relative level of wealth, raising concerns about a persisting digital divide.
sentences.zh: 更深入地观察，从人均角度来看，人工智能的使用情况密切反映了一个国家的相对财富水平，引发了人们对持续存在的数字鸿沟的担忧。

sentences.en: However this isn’t a universal rule: some middle-income countries in South America and the Middle East are adopting AI at rates comparable to higher-income countries.
sentences.zh: 然而，这并不是一个普遍规则：南美和中东的一些中等收入国家采用人工智能的速度与高收入国家相当。

blocks.html_en: Here are some additional findings:

sentences.en: Here are some additional findings:
sentences.zh: 以下是一些额外的发现：

blocks.html_en: How ATLAS has been developed

sentences.en: How ATLAS has been developed
sentences.zh: ATLAS 是如何开发的

blocks.html_en: ATLAS insights are powered by Google DeepMind’s Observation Clustering and Taxonomy Organisation (OCTO), a tool for transforming massive unstructured text data, like LLM conversations, and distilling them into organized entities. ATLAS has been developed with the highest level of privacy protections. In addition to scrubbing personally identifiable information (PII), we added several additional layers of protection that automatically remove any possible references to sensitive information, remove all linkages between de-identified ATLAS data and underlying user logs, summarize the text data, and aggregate summaries into groups representing multiple users.

sentences.en: ATLAS insights are powered by Google DeepMind’s Observation Clustering and Taxonomy Organisation (OCTO), a tool for transforming massive unstructured text data, like LLM conversations, and distilling them into organized entities.
sentences.zh: ATLAS 见解由 Google DeepMind 的观察聚类和分类组织 (OCTO) 提供支持，该工具用于转换大量非结构化文本数据（例如 LLM 对话）并将其提炼为有组织的实体。

sentences.en: ATLAS has been developed with the highest level of privacy protections.
sentences.zh: ATLAS 的开发具有最高级别的隐私保护。

sentences.en: In addition to scrubbing personally identifiable information (PII), we added several additional layers of protection that automatically remove any possible references to sensitive information, remove all linkages between de-identified ATLAS data and underlying user logs, summarize the text data, and aggregate summaries into groups representing multiple users.
sentences.zh: 除了清除个人身份信息 (PII) 之外，我们还添加了几个额外的保护层，这些保护层可以自动删除对敏感信息的任何可能引用、删除去识别化的 ATLAS 数据和底层用户日志之间的所有链接、汇总文本数据并将汇总汇总到代表多个用户的组中。

blocks.html_en: What’s next?

sentences.en: What’s next?
sentences.zh: 接下来是什么？

blocks.html_en: This is just the beginning — the ATLAS v1.0 represents the start of a long-term project. AI’s capabilities continue to expand, people are continuing to find new and interesting ways to use it, and research methodologies to understand AI continue to evolve. There are many more questions around AI and the economy where more work will be needed — work that Google’s AI & Economy Research Program is undertaking in collaboration with academic and other researchers.

sentences.en: This is just the beginning — the ATLAS v1.0 represents the start of a long-term project.
sentences.zh: 这仅仅是一个开始——ATLAS v1.0 代表着一个长期项目的开始。

sentences.en: AI’s capabilities continue to expand, people are continuing to find new and interesting ways to use it, and research methodologies to understand AI continue to evolve.
sentences.zh: 人工智能的能力不断扩展，人们不断寻找新的、有趣的使用方式，理解人工智能的研究方法也在不断发展。

sentences.en: There are many more questions around AI and the economy where more work will be needed — work that Google’s AI & Economy Research Program is undertaking in collaboration with academic and other researchers.
sentences.zh: 关于人工智能和经济还有很多问题需要做更多的工作——谷歌的人工智能和经济研究计划正在与学术界和其他研究人员合作开展这项工作。

blocks.html_en: And there is a much wider range of economically-relevant AI usage not reflected in ATLAS. These include AI-enabled products with billions of users and interactions like Google Workspace, Google Translate, and AI Overviews; enterprise platforms, such as Gemini for Google Cloud and Gemini Enterprise; and frontier capabilities in several key areas, such as agentic coding and world models.

sentences.en: And there is a much wider range of economically-relevant AI usage not reflected in ATLAS.
sentences.zh: 还有更广泛的与经济相关的人工智能应用并未在 ATLAS 中反映出来。

sentences.en: These include AI-enabled products with billions of users and interactions like Google Workspace, Google Translate, and AI Overviews; enterprise platforms, such as Gemini for Google Cloud and Gemini Enterprise; and frontier capabilities in several key areas, such as agentic coding and world models.
sentences.zh: 其中包括拥有数十亿用户的人工智能产品以及 Google Workspace、Google Translate 和 AI Overviews 等交互；企业平台，例如 Gemini for Google Cloud 和 Gemini Enterprise；以及代理编码和世界模型等几个关键领域的前沿能力。

blocks.html_en: As we continue to build upon ATLAS and expand its scope and capabilities to generate new insights, we aim to provide a sharper understanding of the AI-driven transformation of the economy. We hope it is helpful to researchers, policymakers, businesses, workers, and other participants in the economy as we work together to shape the ways AI can positively support people in their lives.

sentences.en: As we continue to build upon ATLAS and expand its scope and capabilities to generate new insights, we aim to provide a sharper understanding of the AI-driven transformation of the economy.
sentences.zh: 随着我们继续以 ATLAS 为基础并扩大其范围和能力以产生新的见解，我们的目标是对人工智能驱动的经济转型提供更清晰的理解。

sentences.en: We hope it is helpful to researchers, policymakers, businesses, workers, and other participants in the economy as we work together to shape the ways AI can positively support people in their lives.
sentences.zh: 我们希望它对研究人员、政策制定者、企业、工人和其他经济参与者有所帮助，因为我们共同努力塑造人工智能积极支持人们生活的方式。

blocks.html_en: We’d like to acknowledge Dame Diane Coyle (Cambridge) and Dr. David Autor (MIT) for their contributions to the ATLAS v1.0 report, as well as the entire ATLAS team 1 .

sentences.en: We’d like to acknowledge Dame Diane Coyle (Cambridge) and Dr. David Autor (MIT) for their contributions to the ATLAS v1.0 report, as well as the entire ATLAS team 1 .
sentences.zh: 我们要感谢 Dame Diane Coyle（剑桥大学）和 David Autor 博士（麻省理工学院）以及整个 ATLAS 团队 1 对 ATLAS v1.0 报告所做的贡献。

blocks.html_en: Get the latest news from Google in your inbox

sentences.en: Get the latest news from Google in your inbox
sentences.zh: 在收件箱中获取来自 Google 的最新新闻

blocks.html_en: Sign up for our newsletters with product updates, event information, special offers, and more.

sentences.en: Sign up for our newsletters with product updates, event information, special offers, and more.
sentences.zh: 订阅我们的时事通讯，了解产品更新、活动信息、特别优惠等。

blocks.html_en: Your information will be used in accordance with Google's privacy policy. You may opt out at any time.

sentences.en: Your information will be used in accordance with Google's privacy policy.
sentences.zh: 您的信息将根据 Google 的隐私政策使用。

sentences.en: You may opt out at any time.
sentences.zh: 您可以随时选择退出。

content_html_zh:
Jul 23, 2026

|

谷歌的 ATLAS 广泛地展示了人们如何在工作和日常生活中使用人工智能。

扎娜·伊斯琴科

首席经济学家办公室人工智能与经济主管

斯科特·斯特兰德

StratOps 和特殊项目、技术与社会主管

您的浏览器不支持音频元素。

听文章

[[持续时间]] 分钟

此内容由 Google AI 生成。 生成式人工智能尚处于实验阶段

人们普遍认为人工智能改变全球经济和我们工作方式的潜力巨大。 然而，其结果 — — 这对工作、人们的生活和经济的意义 — — 并不是自动发生的，也不是必然的。 必须发生很多事情。 为了实现这一目标，我们整个社会必须共同努力，积极塑造人工智能如何影响我们的生活、工作和经济。 为了使这项共同工作发挥作用，深入了解人工智能在经济中的采用和使用方式至关重要。 社会需要经验见解和循证研究来为决策、举措和行动提供信息。

为了提供帮助，谷歌正在启动人工智能与经济 ATLAS（活动、任务、景观和采用研究）的第一次迭代，这是一项持续的、大规模的、去识别化的研究，旨在研究人们如何使用谷歌的人工智能产品和工具。 ATLAS 的第一个数据集 (v1.0) 由 Gemini 应用程序、AI 模式和 Gemini API 中 1500 万个聚合且去识别化的人类与人工智能交互构建而成，每月有超过 10 亿人使用这些交互。 ATLAS v1.0 洞察涵盖 150 多个国家、140 种语言、800 种职业和 4,000 项任务； ATLAS 是迄今为止对真实人们如何大规模使用人工智能的最全面的研究。

ATLAS 揭示了人们如何使用谷歌的人工智能工具来完成工作和日常生活中的各种任务。 ATLAS v1.0 报告提供了快速变化的景观的早期视图：人工智能的能力正在进步，其用途正在不断发展，用于观察其对经济影响的工具仍在开发中。

我们从 ATLAS v1.0 中学到了什么？

以下是迄今为止一些最有趣的观察结果：

  • 人工智能在工作中的应用广泛但肤浅：工作场所的采用涵盖所有行业领域以及所有职业的 68%，这些职业总共占美国总就业人数的 90%。 然而，在工作中，人们有选择性地使用人工智能：在典型的工作中，人工智能仅用于约 21% 的任务。
  • 在工作中，大多数人工智能的使用都集中在任务的协作和协助上，到目前为止，任务自动化并不常见：ATLAS 数据显示，工作中的绝大多数人工智能交互都集中在协作用途，例如构思、策略、信息检索和学习。 创意设计和假设检验（在 ATLAS 中归类为“非常规认知”）等任务在人工智能工作交互中的出现率远高于整个经济中的出现率（65% vs 35%）。 这些交互中只有不到 10% 完全自动化任务。
  • 人工智能的使用不仅限于白领工人，它还帮助主要从事体力和体力职业的工人完成相邻的任务：人工智能在工作中的使用不仅限于传统上被视为知识工作的工作。 虽然不那么普遍，但手工和技术行业的工人（例如汽车技术人员、工业机械师）正在使用对话式人工智能作为实时协作者，进行实时诊断、故障排除和即时学习。 当这些领域的工作人员使用我们的人工智能工具时，他们使用多模式人工智能（即使用人工智能创建图像或视频）的可能性增加了两倍。 例如，汽车技术人员和工业机械师使用人工智能来解释复杂的测试结果、调试电线以及检查机械磨损情况。
  • 人工智能正在家庭中提供标准经济指标可能会忽略的价值，特别是在高摩擦的管理任务方面：超过 86% 的 ATLAS 中与人工智能工具的交互发生在工作之外。 人们正在以标准经济指标中未涵盖的新的、有趣的方式使用人工智能，包括生产性家庭活动（例如研究购买、帮助使用电器和工具）和高摩擦的管理任务（例如处理税收、许可和罚款等政府服务）。
  • 全球人工智能的采用与人均 GDP 挂钩，但有一个值得注意的例外：人工智能的使用已在全球范围内扩散。 ATLAS 数据显示，人工智能在 150 多个国家和地区的使用情况，占世界人口的 99%。 我们在 ATLAS 使用的语言多样性中也看到了这一点。 英语仅占全球人工智能对话的三分之一左右，用户不会系统性地放弃母语来完成复杂的任务。 更深入地观察，从人均角度来看，人工智能的使用情况密切反映了一个国家的相对财富水平，引发了人们对持续存在的数字鸿沟的担忧。 然而，这并不是一个普遍规则：南美和中东的一些中等收入国家采用人工智能的速度与高收入国家相当。

以下是一些额外的发现：

ATLAS 是如何开发的

ATLAS 见解由 Google DeepMind 的观察聚类和分类组织 (OCTO) 提供支持，该工具用于转换大量非结构化文本数据（例如 LLM 对话）并将其提炼为有组织的实体。 ATLAS 的开发具有最高级别的隐私保护。 除了清除个人身份信息 (PII) 之外，我们还添加了几个额外的保护层，这些保护层可以自动删除对敏感信息的任何可能引用、删除去识别化的 ATLAS 数据和底层用户日志之间的所有链接、汇总文本数据并将汇总汇总到代表多个用户的组中。

接下来是什么？

这仅仅是一个开始——ATLAS v1.0 代表着一个长期项目的开始。 人工智能的能力不断扩展，人们不断寻找新的、有趣的使用方式，理解人工智能的研究方法也在不断发展。 关于人工智能和经济还有很多问题需要做更多的工作——谷歌的人工智能和经济研究计划正在与学术界和其他研究人员合作开展这项工作。

还有更广泛的与经济相关的人工智能应用并未在 ATLAS 中反映出来。 其中包括拥有数十亿用户的人工智能产品以及 Google Workspace、Google Translate 和 AI Overviews 等交互；企业平台，例如 Gemini for Google Cloud 和 Gemini Enterprise；以及代理编码和世界模型等几个关键领域的前沿能力。

随着我们继续以 ATLAS 为基础并扩大其范围和能力以产生新的见解，我们的目标是对人工智能驱动的经济转型提供更清晰的理解。 我们希望它对研究人员、政策制定者、企业、工人和其他经济参与者有所帮助，因为我们共同努力塑造人工智能积极支持人们生活的方式。

我们要感谢 Dame Diane Coyle（剑桥大学）和 David Autor 博士（麻省理工学院）以及整个 ATLAS 团队 1 对 ATLAS v1.0 报告所做的贡献。
